strategy.name: @jacktradessss Price Action Strategy
sources: @jacktradessss — YouTube: 'The Trading Industry Will Hate Me for This FREE 10+ Hour Course' (youtu.be/grw58BIzotU)

indicators.ema_50: Trend confluence only — price above EMA50 = bullish bias, below = bearish bias. Secondary confirmation, not a signal itself.
indicators.price_action: Primary signal — Higher Highs / Higher Lows (bullish) or Lower Highs / Lower Lows (bearish) define market structure
indicators.order_blocks: Areas where price previously reversed sharply — used as entry zones on retest
indicators.support_resistance: Historical swing highs/lows used as S&R zones for entries and take profit targets

bullish: Price is making Higher Highs (HH) and Higher Lows (HL) on the higher timeframe
Price is trading above the 50 EMA on the higher timeframe
Most recent swing low held — structure has not broken down
bearish: Price is making Lower Highs (LH) and Lower Lows (LL) on the higher timeframe
Price is trading below the 50 EMA on the higher timeframe
Most recent swing high was rejected — structure has not broken up
neutral: Price is ranging — no clear HH/HL or LH/LL pattern
Price is chopping around the 50 EMA with no direction
Structure is ambiguous — do not trade

long: Step 1: Confirm bullish market structure on higher timeframe (HH + HL pattern)
Step 2: Identify a key order block or support zone that price is approaching
Step 3: Wait for price to break through a recent swing high (break of structure to upside)
Step 4: Wait for price to pull back and retest the broken level or order block
Step 5: Confirm with a candlestick signal at the retest — bullish engulfing, pin bar, or rejection wick
Step 6: Enter at the close of the confirmation candle
short: Step 1: Confirm bearish market structure on higher timeframe (LH + LL pattern)
Step 2: Identify a key order block or resistance zone that price is approaching
Step 3: Wait for price to break through a recent swing low (break of structure to downside)
Step 4: Wait for price to pull back and retest the broken level or order block
Step 5: Confirm with a candlestick signal at the retest — bearish engulfing, pin bar, or rejection wick
Step 6: Enter at the close of the confirmation candle

exit_rules: Take profit at the next significant support/resistance level or swing high/low
Minimum risk:reward of 1:2 before entering any trade — if target does not exist, skip the trade
Exit immediately if price breaks back through the entry structure (trade invalidated)
Move stop loss to breakeven once price reaches 1:1 risk:reward
risk_rules: Risk 1-2% of portfolio per trade maximum
Stop loss placed beyond the order block or structure level that invalidates the trade
Do not move stop loss to a worse position — only trail it to breakeven or better
Do not trade during high-impact news events — wait for the candle to close after news
Preferred trading sessions: Pre-London (1am EST) and London session (3am-12pm EST)
Never trade against the higher timeframe trend — if HTF is bearish, only take shorts
notes: Jack emphasises patience above all else. The setup must be clean — if you have to convince yourself it qualifies, it does not qualify. Only trade the highest-probability breaks and retests where structure, order block, and candlestick confirmation all align. EMA is secondary confluence, not a trigger.
